source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

symbol.description: Filter by ticker, or omit for all

tools.name: getOrders
tools.description:
Query orders by ID. If no orderIds provided, queries all pending (submitted) orders.
Use groupBy: "contract" to group orders by contract/aliceId (useful with many positions + TPSL).
If this tool returns an error with transient=true, wait a few seconds and retry once before reporting to the user.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

orderIds.description: Order IDs to query. If omitted, queries all pending orders.

tools.name: getQuote
tools.description:
Query the latest quote/price for a contract.
If this tool returns an error with transient=true, wait a few seconds and retry once before reporting to the user.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

tools.name: getMarketClock
tools.description:
Get current market clock status (isOpen, nextOpen, nextClose).
If this tool returns an error with transient=true, wait a few seconds and retry once before reporting to the user.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

tools.name: tradingLog
tools.description:
View your trading decision history (like "git log --stat").
IMPORTANT: Check this BEFORE making new trading decisions.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

tools.name: tradingShow
tools.description: View details of a specific trading commit (like "git show <hash>").

tools.name: tradingStatus
tools.description: View current trading staging area status (like "git status").

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

tools.name: placeOrder
tools.description:
Stage an order (will execute on tradingPush).
BEFORE placing orders: check tradingLog, getPortfolio, verify strategy alignment.
NOTE: This stages the operation. Call tradingCommit + tradingPush to execute.
Required params by orderType:
  MKT: totalQuantity (or cashQty)
  LMT: totalQuantity + lmtPrice
  STP: totalQuantity + auxPrice (stop trigger)
  STP LMT: totalQuantity + auxPrice (stop trigger) + lmtPrice
  TRAIL: totalQuantity + auxPrice (trailing offset) or trailingPercent
  TRAIL LIMIT: totalQuantity + auxPrice (trailing offset) + lmtPrice
  MOC: totalQuantity
Optional: attach takeProfit and/or stopLoss for automatic exit orders.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Required for this operation.

totalQuantity.description: Number of shares/contracts as a decimal string (e.g. "0.001"). Mutually exclusive with cashQty.

cashQty.description: Notional dollar amount (mutually exclusive with totalQuantity).

lmtPrice.description: Limit price as a decimal string (required for LMT, STP LMT, TRAIL LIMIT). String preserves satoshi-scale precision.

auxPrice.description: Stop trigger price for STP/STP LMT; trailing offset amount for TRAIL/TRAIL LIMIT.

trailStopPrice.description: Initial trailing stop price (TRAIL/TRAIL LIMIT only).

trailingPercent.description: Trailing stop percentage offset (alternative to auxPrice for TRAIL).

outsideRth.description: Allow execution outside regular trading hours

limitPrice.description: Limit price for stop-limit SL (omit for stop-market)

tools.name: modifyOrder
tools.description:
Stage an order modification.
NOTE: This stages the operation. Call tradingCommit + tradingPush to execute.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Required for this operation.

auxPrice.description: New stop trigger price or trailing offset (depends on order type). Decimal string.

trailStopPrice.description: New initial trailing stop price. Decimal string.

trailingPercent.description: New trailing stop percentage. Decimal string.

tools.name: closePosition
tools.description:
Stage a position close.
NOTE: This stages the operation. Call tradingCommit + tradingPush to execute.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Required for this operation.

qty.description: Number of shares to sell. Decimal string. Default: sell all.

tools.name: cancelOrder
tools.description:
Stage an order cancellation.
NOTE: This stages the operation. Call tradingCommit + tradingPush to execute.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Required for this operation.

tools.name: tradingCommit
tools.description: Commit staged trading operations with a message (like "git commit -m"). Does NOT execute yet.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts. If omitted, commits all accounts with staged operations.

message.description: Commit message explaining your trading decision

tools.name: tradingPush
tools.description:
Push committed operations to the broker. AUTO-EXECUTES (no human approval gate).
Trades have already passed the standing-orders authorization upstream;
the human-in-the-loop check at this layer is intentionally disabled —
the agent IS the operator and is expected to push its own decisions.

Only call this AFTER tradingCommit. Returns per-account push results
including filled, rejected, and any broker errors.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts. If omitted, pushes all accounts with pending commits.

tools.name: tradingSync
tools.description: Sync pending order statuses from broker (like "git pull"). Use delayMs to wait before querying — exchanges may need a few seconds to settle after order placement.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts. If omitted, syncs all accounts with pending orders.

tools.name: getFrontalLobe
tools.description:
Read your previous personal notes — things YOU committed to in earlier rounds.

These are *your* rules, attention targets, self-constraints, and operating
stances, not facts about the market. They persist across rounds so you can
maintain continuity in what you've decided to do, what you're watching for,
and the frame you've been working under.

Use this at the start of a round to recover the context of your own prior
decisions — what rules are still armed, what events you were waiting for,
what constraints you put on yourself.

Returns: your notes as a string (empty if nothing is set yet).

tools.name: updateFrontalLobe
tools.description:
Update your personal notes — things that belong to YOU, not to the world.

These notes persist across rounds, so the rule is strict: write only content
that the world cannot falsify. World-referring content goes stale between
rounds and will pollute your future reasoning.

✅ Write:
- Conditional rules you've committed to: "If ETH reclaims 3800, add 10%"
- Events you're waiting on: "FOMC Thursday 14:00 — check policy signal"
- Self-constraints: "No new entries until macro clarity"
- Operating stances: "Running under consolidation assumption for BTC range"

❌ Do NOT write:
- Market state: "Market is in strong uptrend" — will be false by next round
- Derivable facts: "Position is +15% PnL" — query the tool instead
- Predictions: "BTC should reach 100k" — pollutes next round's reasoning
- Emotion/confidence: "Feeling confident about this" — not decision-relevant

Your notes replace the previous ones entirely each time — drop rules that
fired or expired, carry forward ones still active, add new ones.

Example (all self-referring, no world claims):
"If ETH reclaims 3800, add 10% to the long. Waiting on FOMC Thursday 14:00.
Holding off new entries until macro clarity. Running under consolidation
assumption for BTC range — flip if we break 98k or lose 94k."

content.description: Your personal notes (rules, attention targets, self-constraints, stances — not facts or predictions)

tools.name: calculateIndicator
tools.description:
Calculate technical indicators for any asset using formula expressions.

Asset classes: "equity" for stocks, "crypto" for cryptocurrencies, "currency" for forex pairs, "commodity" for commodities (use canonical names: gold, crude_oil, copper, etc.).

Data access (returns array — use [-1] for latest value):
  CLOSE('AAPL', '1d'), HIGH, LOW, OPEN, VOLUME — args: symbol, interval (e.g. '1d', '1w', '1h').
  CLOSE('AAPL', '1d')[-1] → latest close price as a single number.

Statistics (returns a single number — do NOT use [-1]):
  SMA(data, period), EMA, STDEV, MAX, MIN, SUM, AVERAGE.

Technical (returns a single number or object — do NOT use [-1]):
  RSI(data, 14) → number.  BBANDS(data, 20, 2) → {upper, middle, lower}.
  MACD(data, 12, 26, 9) → {macd, signal, histogram}.  ATR(highs, lows, closes, 14) → number.

Arithmetic: +, -, *, / operators between numbers. E.g. CLOSE(...)[-1] - SMA(..., 50).

Examples:
  SMA(CLOSE('AAPL', '1d'), 50)              → equity 50-day moving average
  RSI(CLOSE('BTCUSD', '1d'), 14)            → crypto RSI (single number, no [-1])
  CLOSE('EURUSD', '1d')[-1]                 → latest forex close (needs [-1])
  CLOSE('gold', '1d')[-1]                   → latest gold price (canonical name)

Returns { value, dataRange } where dataRange shows the actual date span of the data used.
Use marketSearchForResearch to find the correct symbol first.

tools.name: volumeScanner
tools.description:
Scan equities for volume anomalies. HYDRA-ported thresholds:
- ratio >= 2.0x 20-day average volume (accumulation)
- price > $5
- avg volume > 100K
Returns top candidates with their volume ratio, 5-day return, RSI, and sector.
Use this for the runner strategy. Combine with sectorBreakoutDetector + regimeClassifier
before sizing entries. Universe is the static large-cap list.

tools.name: regimeClassifier
tools.description:
Classify SPY into bull / neutral / bear via 5-day backtest-validated
rules (Sharpe 1.406, max DD 8.55%, beats 20-day Sharpe 1.303 on 2024-05 → 2026-05 SPY):
- return_5d  > 0.03  -> bull (multiplier 1.0)
- return_5d  < -0.03 -> bear (0.125)
- realized_vol_5d < 0.15 and |return_5d| <= 0.03 -> bull (1.0)
- otherwise -> neutral (0.5)
Returns the regime, multiplier, and the two raw features (legacy field names
return20d/realizedVol20d kept for UI compatibility — values are now 5-day).

tools.name: atrStopManager
tools.description:
Check whether positions have breached their ATR(14) trailing stops.
Returns breach status + stop level per position. Period 14, default multiplier 2.5
(3.0 for crypto). Caller passes the symbol + entry context.

PREFERRED bulk usage:
  atrStopManager({ positions: [
    { symbol: 'AAPL', entryPrice: 150, peakPrice: 165, side: 'long' },
    { symbol: 'BTCUSD', entryPrice: 60000, peakPrice: 62000, accountId: 'kraken-paper', atrMultiplier: 3.0 },
    ...
  ] })
→ returns { results: [...], summary: { breached: ['AAPL'], ... } }

Single-position legacy usage:
  atrStopManager({ symbol, entryPrice, peakPrice, side, accountId? })
→ returns the per-position result directly.

For crypto positions on `kraken-paper`, pass `accountId: 'kraken-paper'`
so the data path routes to Kraken's public OHLC instead of the equity client
(which would resolve "DASH" to DoorDash, "BTC" to Grayscale Bitcoin ETF, etc.).

positions.description: Bulk mode — array of positions. Preferred when scanning >1 position.

accountId.description: Account hint for data routing. "kraken-paper" → Kraken OHLC; otherwise equity client.

tools.name: sectorBreakoutDetector
tools.description:
Detect sector ETFs breaking 5-day high on 1.5x volume confirmation.
Backtested: 73% win rate, +1.83% avg 10d return, Sharpe 0.535. 13 ETFs:
the 11 SPDR sectors plus SMH and SOXX (semi sub-sector). Returns active
breakouts sorted by volume strength.

tools.name: ibsSignal
tools.description:
IBS mean-reversion signal (QQQ default).
ENTRY when: IBS < 0.2 AND RSI(2) < 10 AND close > SMA(200).
EXIT when: IBS > 0.8.
Returns the signal with feature values. Hard limit: max 10-day hold (caller enforces).
2026-06-09: reverted to 0.2/10 (the validated thresholds). The 0.3/15 loosening
of 2026-05-08 was a mistake — see Gate-Change Policy in standing-orders.md.
Diluting a gate to generate activity is how edges die. Low fire rate is a
feature, not a defect.

tools.name: sectorMomentumRanker
tools.description:
Rank 11 SPDR sector ETFs by 20-day return. For each top-N sector,
return the best stock in that sector by 10-day momentum. Used by the
concentrated strategy on its monthly first-Friday rebalance. Caller enforces
sector uniqueness.

topN.description: Top N sectors to return picks for

tools.name: vrpSpreadFinder
tools.description:
Find a SPY put-credit-spread using the REAL Alpaca options chain.
Picks a short put near the target delta (25 by default) and a long put
"strikeWidth" strikes lower for protection. Quotes come from Alpaca's
snapshots endpoint (indicative outside hours, OPRA-tagged inside hours).

Returns actual contract symbols (e.g. SPY260620P00700000), live bid/ask,
mid-price credit, max loss, breakeven, DTE. The caller places the two
legs via the trading tool against alpaca-paper.

targetDelta.description: Target delta for short put (absolute value)

strikeWidth.description: Distance between short and long put strikes ($)

tools.name: peadScanner
tools.description:
Post-Earnings Announcement Drift screener.

Pulls the Nasdaq earnings calendar (no API key required) for the last
windowDays, calculates Standardized Unexpected Earnings (SUE) per row
with both an actual and a forecast EPS, and returns rows where
|SUE| > threshold sorted strongest-first.

SUE = (actual - estimate) / |estimate|. Standing orders open longs on
SUE > 0.5 with 60-day hold; the negative side is informational (PEAD
short legs were not part of HYDRA's backtested rules).

windowDays.description: How many days back to scan

tools.name: overnightDriftSignal
tools.description:
Overnight drift signal — buy at close after qualifying down days, sell at next open.
Entry: today's return < -0.5%, close > SMA(200), volume > 20d avg.
Returns ENTRY / NO_SIGNAL with the underlying readings. DISABLED by default;
enable after forward-evaluating the signal on paper.

downThreshold.description: Today return must be below this (e.g. -0.005 = -0.5%)

tools.name: pairsTradeScanner
tools.description:
Cointegration pairs-trade scanner across 10 large-cap pairs.
Pulls daily closes, fits hedge ratio via OLS, computes the spread's z-score
vs its lookbackDays mean/std. Entry: |z| >= zScoreEntry. Exit: |z| <= zScoreExit.
Direction: short A / long B when z > 0; reverse when z < 0. DISABLED by default.

tools.name: zdteCondorScanner
tools.description:
Find a same-day SPY iron condor using the live Alpaca options chain.
Pulls today's expiry contracts, picks short call/put at +/- wingDistance% from spot,
buy long call/put wingWidth $ further out for protection. Returns 4 leg symbols,
live bid/ask/mid for each, total credit, max loss, breakeven range, and a
TRADE/SKIP recommendation.

Returns status='no-expiry' if today is not a SPY expiry day (most weekdays are,
but holidays + non-Mon/Wed/Fri historically had gaps; SPY now has daily expiries).

wingDistance.description: Short-strike distance from spot, in % (e.g. 1.0 = ±1% strikes).

wingWidth.description: Distance between short and long strike in $ (5 = 5-wide protective wings).

minCreditFraction.description: Minimum total credit / wingWidth ratio to recommend TRADE. 0.15 = 15%; the conservative-scenario threshold from the backtest.

tools.name: etfRotation
tools.description:
Monthly sector-ETF rotation — ranks sector ETFs by 20-day return and applies a regime filter.
Bull regime: top-N long sectors (XLK/XLF/XLE/...). Neutral: top-2 long + 1 bond ETF (BND).
Bear: top-2 inverse ETFs (SH/PSQ) + 1 bond. Companion to concentrated which picks individual
stocks; this picks the diversified ETF for the same sector momentum signal.

topN.description: How many ETFs to hold (default 2; backtest matrix Sharpe peak).

lookbackDays.description: Return lookback window (default 10 trading days; backtest Sharpe 1.41 top-2).

includeInverse.description: Include inverse ETFs (SH/PSQ/SQQQ/SPXU) for bear-regime hedges.

tools.name: cryptoRankingMomentum
tools.description:
Crypto relative-momentum scanner — buys coins climbing the rankings.
Reads top-100 coins by market cap from CoinGecko, ranks them by 7-day
return, returns the top-N (default 5) with their live price, mcap, and
returns. Skips stablecoins and wrapped tokens. Caller pipes through
positionSizer({ strategy: 'crypto_ranking', accountId: 'kraken-paper' }).
Backtest: +295.98% / Sharpe 0.83 / WR 35.8% / 204 trades over 12 months
(beat BTC HODL by ~318 pp).

topUniverseN.description: How many top-mcap coins to scan (CoinGecko returns 100 in one call).

holdings.description: How many top-by-return coins to return as candidates.

lookbackDays.description: Lookback window in days for ranking by return (default 7 = backward-compatible; sweep best was 10).

minReturn7d.description: Floor on lookback return (decimal). Defaults to 0 — accept any positive momentum. Field name kept for back-compat.

tools.name: equityMomentumScanner
tools.description:
Equity 5-day momentum scanner — bull-regime entries on the curated large-cap universe.
ENTRY when:
- 5-day return >= minReturn5d (default 3%)
- 10-day return > 0 (direction confirms)
- close > SMA(200) (uptrend filter)
- RSI(14) < maxRsi (default 75; avoid overbought)
- volume > 20-day avg (above-average participation)
- close > $5 (no penny stocks)
Returns candidates ranked by 5-day return descending. Caller layers
regime gate (bull-only) and ATR trailing stop.

symbols.description: Explicit symbol override. When provided, replaces the curated universe.

tools.name: equityMacdScanner
tools.description:
Equity MACD-crossover scanner. ENTRY when MACD(12,26,9) crosses above signal AND hist > 0 AND close > EMA(200).
Universe defaults to the curated ~120 large-cap RUNNER_UNIVERSE.
Returns ranked candidates. Caller layers regime gate (bull-only) and ATR trail.
Backtest: +32.59% / Sharpe 1.24 / WR 46.8% / 139 trades / 28 months / 1173 stocks (auto-quant).

symbols.description: Override the curated universe with explicit symbols.

tools.name: equityDonchianScanner
tools.description:
Equity Donchian (Turtle) breakout scanner. ENTRY when close > prior 20-day highest high.
Universe defaults to the curated RUNNER_UNIVERSE. Returns ranked candidates.
Caller layers regime gate (bull-only) and ATR trail. Exit guidance: close < 10-day low.
Backtest: +67.80% / Sharpe 0.88 / WR 41.5% / 195 trades / 28 months / 1173 stocks (auto-quant).

tools.name: equityIchimokuScanner
tools.description:
Equity Ichimoku Cloud TK-cross scanner. ENTRY when tenkan(9) crosses above kijun(26) AND close is above the cloud (max of senkouA/B plotted 26 bars ago).
Universe defaults to the curated RUNNER_UNIVERSE. Long-only.
Caller layers regime gate (bull-only) and exits when TK cross reverses.
Backtest: +29.81% / Sharpe 1.49 (ensemble period) / WR 46.5% / 129 trades / 1173 stocks (auto-quant).

symbols.description: Override the curated universe with explicit symbols.

tools.name: rsi2MeanReversion
tools.description:
RSI(2) extreme mean-reversion scanner. ENTRY when RSI(2) < entryThreshold AND close > EMA(200) (uptrend filter).
Returns ranked candidates (most oversold first). Caller stages a limit buy + sets a 1.5× ATR stop and exits when RSI(2) > exitThreshold or 10 days pass.
Backtest: Sharpe 0.76 / +0.95% avg / 38,916 trades / 1.5× ATR stop critical.

symbols.description: Override the curated universe with explicit symbols.

tools.name: insiderBuyingTracker
tools.description: Track recent insider buying (Form 4 purchases via openinsider.com). C-suite + Directors only, $100K minimum. Returns ranked signals with confidence-boost classification: LARGE_CLUSTER / LARGE / CLUSTER / SINGLE. Backtest: +3.31% alpha at 30d (t=6.95) on 3,454 signals. Use as confirmation overlay, not a standalone trigger.

daysBack.description: Lookback window in days for filings.

largeThreshold.description: Threshold above which a buy is classified LARGE.

tools.name: gapFadeFilter
tools.description: Pre-market scan for stocks gapping up >= 15%. Backtest: these fade 62% of the time. Returns a list of symbols to AVOID buying today. Defensive filter, NOT a trading signal. Persists to data/gap-flags.json so the confidence engine penalises flagged names automatically. Run at 08:00 ET and 09:35 ET daily.

tools.name: rankSignals
tools.description:
Rank pending entry signals by expected value and apply concentration filters.

INPUT: an array of { symbol, strategy, details? } from any scanner.
OUTPUT: { accepted, rejected, ranked, summary } — accepted is the list
to actually stage entries for, in priority order. rejected explains why
each non-accepted candidate was filtered (industry-group, strategy-cap,
gap-fade-flag, regime-bear-max, equity-total-cap, or crypto-separate-pool).

Strategy EV ranking (highest first): concentrated > etf_rotation > equity_donchian
> equity_macd > rsi2_reversion > equity_ichimoku > pead > overnight_drift.

Use this AFTER calling all scanners and BEFORE staging any entries.

signals.description: All pending signals from all scanners. Tag each with the originating strategy.

currentPositions.description: Live position list. If omitted, reads from data/state/strategy-positions.json.

regime.description: Current regime. If omitted, reads lastRegime from data/state/pulse-state.json.

tools.name: positionSizer
tools.description:
Compute the exact position size for a trade. Call this BEFORE staging any order.
Equities draw from a shared $4K cash pool (per-position base = available cash / remaining slots
for the strategy). Crypto uses a separate $1K Kraken pool with 8 slots. Returns dollar amount,
share count, full sizing breakdown, and a human rationale. If `status === 'rejected'`, do not
place the trade.

strategy.description: Which strategy this trade belongs to. Drives slot count and Kelly fraction.

accountId.description: Account to size against. Equities default to alpaca-paper, crypto to kraken-paper.

tools.name: narrativeWeekly
tools.description: Rebuild the narrative dislocation map. Identifies confirmed themes (9B), traverses causal chains (Sonnet), verifies + flow/attention-filters via code, writes data/state/narrative-map.json + data/brain/narrative-journal.md. Should run weekly Sunday 22 UTC.

tools.name: narrativeDaily
tools.description: Apply the narrative map to the paper book: refresh entry/exit decisions for up to 8 paper positions, $150 each. Exits on attention arrival (>5 co-mentions), flow gap close (>=80%), theme off map, -15% stop, or 60d max hold. Records closed trades in the trade ledger with strategy='narrative'.

tools.name: narrativeMap
tools.description: Read the current narrative dislocation map + paper book. Use for status checks without rebuilding the map.

symbol.description: If set, return only the candidate row for this symbol (cross-confirmation lookup).

tools.name: strategyAttribution
tools.description:
Per-strategy realized P&L over a trailing window.
- action='read' → group existing ledger rows by strategy (no network)
- action='reconcile' → pull new Alpaca FILL activities + extend ledger
- action='full' (default) → reconcile then read

Returns: { windowDays, strategies: [{strategy, trades, realizedPnL, winRate, avgHoldDays}], totalRealized, untaggedPnL, reconcileStats?}.
Untagged P&L is itself a diagnostic — code paths that bypass strategy tagging show up here.

accountId.description: Only alpaca-paper is supported for the reconcile step today; read covers all accounts.

tools.name: systemStatus
tools.description:
Server hardware status: CPU, memory, disk, uptime, load average, GPU, running containers, and key systemd services.
No inputs. Returns a snapshot of host health.

tools.name: ollamaStatus
tools.description:
Ollama health: version, currently loaded model(s) with VRAM usage, available models in catalog, and the OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL setting.
Returns status='offline' if the server can't be reached.

tools.name: processHealth
tools.description: Health of Hydra-related processes: openalice + ollama + kraken + nginx, with PID, RSS memory, and recent errors from journalctl. Plus disk usage of key data directories.

tools.name: newsSentimentScanner
tools.description:
Score news headlines for stocks via Ollama, aggregate to sectors.

Modes:
  - 'full' (default): score the ~150 scheduledScoring symbols, write data/sentiment.json. ~3min.
  - 'single': score ONE symbol on demand (e.g. when MACD/Donchian fires on a name not in the 150). ~3s. Persists.
  - 'sector': score every symbol in one ETF bucket. ~3-5min depending on bucket size. Persists.
  - 'all':    score every symbol in the 1173 universe. ~20min. Persists.

Reads sector mapping from data/sector-universe.json (built by scripts/build_sector_universe.py).

symbol.description: Required when mode=single — the ticker to score.

etf.description: Required when mode=sector — the SPDR ETF (XLK, XLF, ...) of the sector to scan.

tools.name: calculate
tools.description: Perform mathematical calculations with precision. Use this for any arithmetic operations instead of calculating yourself. Supports basic operators: +, -, *, /, (), decimals.

tools.name: listUTAs
tools.description: List all registered trading accounts with their id, provider, label, and capabilities.

tools.name: searchContracts
tools.description:
Search broker accounts for tradeable contracts matching a pattern.
This is a BROKER-LEVEL search — it queries your connected trading accounts.

Pass `assetClass` when known (especially "crypto" or "currency") so the
data-vendor symbol is normalized into a broker-friendly pattern — e.g. a
search for "BTCUSD" with assetClass="crypto" is rewritten to "BTC" before
hitting the broker, which otherwise expects the bare base ticker.

assetClass.description: Asset class hint. Improves matching for crypto/currency where data symbols concatenate quote currency.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

tools.name: getContractDetails
tools.description: Get full contract specification from a specific broker account.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Required for this operation.

tools.name: getAccount
tools.description:
Query trading account info (netLiquidation, totalCashValue, buyingPower, unrealizedPnL, realizedPnL).
If this tool returns an error with transient=true, wait a few seconds and retry once before reporting to the user.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

tools.name: getPortfolio
tools.description:
Query current portfolio holdings. IMPORTANT: If result is an empty array [], you have no holdings.
If this tool returns an error with transient=true, wait a few seconds and retry once before reporting to the user.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

symbol.description: Filter by ticker, or omit for all

tools.name: getOrders
tools.description:
Query orders by ID. If no orderIds provided, queries all pending (submitted) orders.
Use groupBy: "contract" to group orders by contract/aliceId (useful with many positions + TPSL).
If this tool returns an error with transient=true, wait a few seconds and retry once before reporting to the user.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

orderIds.description: Order IDs to query. If omitted, queries all pending orders.

tools.name: getQuote
tools.description:
Query the latest quote/price for a contract.
If this tool returns an error with transient=true, wait a few seconds and retry once before reporting to the user.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

tools.name: getMarketClock
tools.description:
Get current market clock status (isOpen, nextOpen, nextClose).
If this tool returns an error with transient=true, wait a few seconds and retry once before reporting to the user.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

tools.name: tradingLog
tools.description:
View your trading decision history (like "git log --stat").
IMPORTANT: Check this BEFORE making new trading decisions.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

tools.name: tradingShow
tools.description: View details of a specific trading commit (like "git show <hash>").

tools.name: tradingStatus
tools.description: View current trading staging area status (like "git status").

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts.

tools.name: placeOrder
tools.description:
Stage an order (will execute on tradingPush).
BEFORE placing orders: check tradingLog, getPortfolio, verify strategy alignment.
NOTE: This stages the operation. Call tradingCommit + tradingPush to execute.
Required params by orderType:
  MKT: totalQuantity (or cashQty)
  LMT: totalQuantity + lmtPrice
  STP: totalQuantity + auxPrice (stop trigger)
  STP LMT: totalQuantity + auxPrice (stop trigger) + lmtPrice
  TRAIL: totalQuantity + auxPrice (trailing offset) or trailingPercent
  TRAIL LIMIT: totalQuantity + auxPrice (trailing offset) + lmtPrice
  MOC: totalQuantity
Optional: attach takeProfit and/or stopLoss for automatic exit orders.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Required for this operation.

totalQuantity.description: Number of shares/contracts as a decimal string (e.g. "0.001"). Mutually exclusive with cashQty.

cashQty.description: Notional dollar amount (mutually exclusive with totalQuantity).

lmtPrice.description: Limit price as a decimal string (required for LMT, STP LMT, TRAIL LIMIT). String preserves satoshi-scale precision.

auxPrice.description: Stop trigger price for STP/STP LMT; trailing offset amount for TRAIL/TRAIL LIMIT.

trailStopPrice.description: Initial trailing stop price (TRAIL/TRAIL LIMIT only).

trailingPercent.description: Trailing stop percentage offset (alternative to auxPrice for TRAIL).

outsideRth.description: Allow execution outside regular trading hours

limitPrice.description: Limit price for stop-limit SL (omit for stop-market)

tools.name: modifyOrder
tools.description:
Stage an order modification.
NOTE: This stages the operation. Call tradingCommit + tradingPush to execute.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Required for this operation.

auxPrice.description: New stop trigger price or trailing offset (depends on order type). Decimal string.

trailStopPrice.description: New initial trailing stop price. Decimal string.

trailingPercent.description: New trailing stop percentage. Decimal string.

tools.name: closePosition
tools.description:
Stage a position close.
NOTE: This stages the operation. Call tradingCommit + tradingPush to execute.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Required for this operation.

qty.description: Number of shares to sell. Decimal string. Default: sell all.

tools.name: cancelOrder
tools.description:
Stage an order cancellation.
NOTE: This stages the operation. Call tradingCommit + tradingPush to execute.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Required for this operation.

tools.name: tradingCommit
tools.description: Commit staged trading operations with a message (like "git commit -m"). Does NOT execute yet.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts. If omitted, commits all accounts with staged operations.

message.description: Commit message explaining your trading decision

tools.name: tradingPush
tools.description:
Push committed operations to the broker. AUTO-EXECUTES (no human approval gate).
Trades have already passed the standing-orders authorization upstream;
the human-in-the-loop check at this layer is intentionally disabled —
the agent IS the operator and is expected to push its own decisions.

Only call this AFTER tradingCommit. Returns per-account push results
including filled, rejected, and any broker errors.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts. If omitted, pushes all accounts with pending commits.

tools.name: tradingSync
tools.description: Sync pending order statuses from broker (like "git pull"). Use delayMs to wait before querying — exchanges may need a few seconds to settle after order placement.

source.description: Account source — matches account id (e.g. "alpaca-paper") or provider (e.g. "alpaca", "ccxt"). Optional — omit to query all accounts. If omitted, syncs all accounts with pending orders.

tools.name: getFrontalLobe
tools.description:
Read your previous personal notes — things YOU committed to in earlier rounds.

These are *your* rules, attention targets, self-constraints, and operating
stances, not facts about the market. They persist across rounds so you can
maintain continuity in what you've decided to do, what you're watching for,
and the frame you've been working under.

Use this at the start of a round to recover the context of your own prior
decisions — what rules are still armed, what events you were waiting for,
what constraints you put on yourself.

Returns: your notes as a string (empty if nothing is set yet).

tools.name: updateFrontalLobe
tools.description:
Update your personal notes — things that belong to YOU, not to the world.

These notes persist across rounds, so the rule is strict: write only content
that the world cannot falsify. World-referring content goes stale between
rounds and will pollute your future reasoning.

✅ Write:
- Conditional rules you've committed to: "If ETH reclaims 3800, add 10%"
- Events you're waiting on: "FOMC Thursday 14:00 — check policy signal"
- Self-constraints: "No new entries until macro clarity"
- Operating stances: "Running under consolidation assumption for BTC range"

❌ Do NOT write:
- Market state: "Market is in strong uptrend" — will be false by next round
- Derivable facts: "Position is +15% PnL" — query the tool instead
- Predictions: "BTC should reach 100k" — pollutes next round's reasoning
- Emotion/confidence: "Feeling confident about this" — not decision-relevant

Your notes replace the previous ones entirely each time — drop rules that
fired or expired, carry forward ones still active, add new ones.

Example (all self-referring, no world claims):
"If ETH reclaims 3800, add 10% to the long. Waiting on FOMC Thursday 14:00.
Holding off new entries until macro clarity. Running under consolidation
assumption for BTC range — flip if we break 98k or lose 94k."

content.description: Your personal notes (rules, attention targets, self-constraints, stances — not facts or predictions)

tools.name: calculateIndicator
tools.description:
Calculate technical indicators for any asset using formula expressions.

Asset classes: "equity" for stocks, "crypto" for cryptocurrencies, "currency" for forex pairs, "commodity" for commodities (use canonical names: gold, crude_oil, copper, etc.).

Data access (returns array — use [-1] for latest value):
  CLOSE('AAPL', '1d'), HIGH, LOW, OPEN, VOLUME — args: symbol, interval (e.g. '1d', '1w', '1h').
  CLOSE('AAPL', '1d')[-1] → latest close price as a single number.

Statistics (returns a single number — do NOT use [-1]):
  SMA(data, period), EMA, STDEV, MAX, MIN, SUM, AVERAGE.

Technical (returns a single number or object — do NOT use [-1]):
  RSI(data, 14) → number.  BBANDS(data, 20, 2) → {upper, middle, lower}.
  MACD(data, 12, 26, 9) → {macd, signal, histogram}.  ATR(highs, lows, closes, 14) → number.

Arithmetic: +, -, *, / operators between numbers. E.g. CLOSE(...)[-1] - SMA(..., 50).

Examples:
  SMA(CLOSE('AAPL', '1d'), 50)              → equity 50-day moving average
  RSI(CLOSE('BTCUSD', '1d'), 14)            → crypto RSI (single number, no [-1])
  CLOSE('EURUSD', '1d')[-1]                 → latest forex close (needs [-1])
  CLOSE('gold', '1d')[-1]                   → latest gold price (canonical name)

Returns { value, dataRange } where dataRange shows the actual date span of the data used.
Use marketSearchForResearch to find the correct symbol first.

tools.name: volumeScanner
tools.description:
Scan equities for volume anomalies. HYDRA-ported thresholds:
- ratio >= 2.0x 20-day average volume (accumulation)
- price > $5
- avg volume > 100K
Returns top candidates with their volume ratio, 5-day return, RSI, and sector.
Use this for the runner strategy. Combine with sectorBreakoutDetector + regimeClassifier
before sizing entries. Universe is the static large-cap list.

tools.name: regimeClassifier
tools.description:
Classify SPY into bull / neutral / bear via 5-day backtest-validated
rules (Sharpe 1.406, max DD 8.55%, beats 20-day Sharpe 1.303 on 2024-05 → 2026-05 SPY):
- return_5d  > 0.03  -> bull (multiplier 1.0)
- return_5d  < -0.03 -> bear (0.125)
- realized_vol_5d < 0.15 and |return_5d| <= 0.03 -> bull (1.0)
- otherwise -> neutral (0.5)
Returns the regime, multiplier, and the two raw features (legacy field names
return20d/realizedVol20d kept for UI compatibility — values are now 5-day).

tools.name: atrStopManager
tools.description:
Check whether positions have breached their ATR(14) trailing stops.
Returns breach status + stop level per position. Period 14, default multiplier 2.5
(3.0 for crypto). Caller passes the symbol + entry context.

PREFERRED bulk usage:
  atrStopManager({ positions: [
    { symbol: 'AAPL', entryPrice: 150, peakPrice: 165, side: 'long' },
    { symbol: 'BTCUSD', entryPrice: 60000, peakPrice: 62000, accountId: 'kraken-paper', atrMultiplier: 3.0 },
    ...
  ] })
→ returns { results: [...], summary: { breached: ['AAPL'], ... } }

Single-position legacy usage:
  atrStopManager({ symbol, entryPrice, peakPrice, side, accountId? })
→ returns the per-position result directly.

For crypto positions on `kraken-paper`, pass `accountId: 'kraken-paper'`
so the data path routes to Kraken's public OHLC instead of the equity client
(which would resolve "DASH" to DoorDash, "BTC" to Grayscale Bitcoin ETF, etc.).

positions.description: Bulk mode — array of positions. Preferred when scanning >1 position.

accountId.description: Account hint for data routing. "kraken-paper" → Kraken OHLC; otherwise equity client.

tools.name: sectorBreakoutDetector
tools.description:
Detect sector ETFs breaking 5-day high on 1.5x volume confirmation.
Backtested: 73% win rate, +1.83% avg 10d return, Sharpe 0.535. 13 ETFs:
the 11 SPDR sectors plus SMH and SOXX (semi sub-sector). Returns active
breakouts sorted by volume strength.

tools.name: ibsSignal
tools.description:
IBS mean-reversion signal (QQQ default).
ENTRY when: IBS < 0.2 AND RSI(2) < 10 AND close > SMA(200).
EXIT when: IBS > 0.8.
Returns the signal with feature values. Hard limit: max 10-day hold (caller enforces).
2026-06-09: reverted to 0.2/10 (the validated thresholds). The 0.3/15 loosening
of 2026-05-08 was a mistake — see Gate-Change Policy in standing-orders.md.
Diluting a gate to generate activity is how edges die. Low fire rate is a
feature, not a defect.

tools.name: sectorMomentumRanker
tools.description:
Rank 11 SPDR sector ETFs by 20-day return. For each top-N sector,
return the best stock in that sector by 10-day momentum. Used by the
concentrated strategy on its monthly first-Friday rebalance. Caller enforces
sector uniqueness.

topN.description: Top N sectors to return picks for

tools.name: vrpSpreadFinder
tools.description:
Find a SPY put-credit-spread using the REAL Alpaca options chain.
Picks a short put near the target delta (25 by default) and a long put
"strikeWidth" strikes lower for protection. Quotes come from Alpaca's
snapshots endpoint (indicative outside hours, OPRA-tagged inside hours).

Returns actual contract symbols (e.g. SPY260620P00700000), live bid/ask,
mid-price credit, max loss, breakeven, DTE. The caller places the two
legs via the trading tool against alpaca-paper.

targetDelta.description: Target delta for short put (absolute value)

strikeWidth.description: Distance between short and long put strikes ($)

tools.name: peadScanner
tools.description:
Post-Earnings Announcement Drift screener.

Pulls the Nasdaq earnings calendar (no API key required) for the last
windowDays, calculates Standardized Unexpected Earnings (SUE) per row
with both an actual and a forecast EPS, and returns rows where
|SUE| > threshold sorted strongest-first.

SUE = (actual - estimate) / |estimate|. Standing orders open longs on
SUE > 0.5 with 60-day hold; the negative side is informational (PEAD
short legs were not part of HYDRA's backtested rules).

windowDays.description: How many days back to scan

tools.name: overnightDriftSignal
tools.description:
Overnight drift signal — buy at close after qualifying down days, sell at next open.
Entry: today's return < -0.5%, close > SMA(200), volume > 20d avg.
Returns ENTRY / NO_SIGNAL with the underlying readings. DISABLED by default;
enable after forward-evaluating the signal on paper.

downThreshold.description: Today return must be below this (e.g. -0.005 = -0.5%)

tools.name: pairsTradeScanner
tools.description:
Cointegration pairs-trade scanner across 10 large-cap pairs.
Pulls daily closes, fits hedge ratio via OLS, computes the spread's z-score
vs its lookbackDays mean/std. Entry: |z| >= zScoreEntry. Exit: |z| <= zScoreExit.
Direction: short A / long B when z > 0; reverse when z < 0. DISABLED by default.

tools.name: zdteCondorScanner
tools.description:
Find a same-day SPY iron condor using the live Alpaca options chain.
Pulls today's expiry contracts, picks short call/put at +/- wingDistance% from spot,
buy long call/put wingWidth $ further out for protection. Returns 4 leg symbols,
live bid/ask/mid for each, total credit, max loss, breakeven range, and a
TRADE/SKIP recommendation.

Returns status='no-expiry' if today is not a SPY expiry day (most weekdays are,
but holidays + non-Mon/Wed/Fri historically had gaps; SPY now has daily expiries).

wingDistance.description: Short-strike distance from spot, in % (e.g. 1.0 = ±1% strikes).

wingWidth.description: Distance between short and long strike in $ (5 = 5-wide protective wings).

minCreditFraction.description: Minimum total credit / wingWidth ratio to recommend TRADE. 0.15 = 15%; the conservative-scenario threshold from the backtest.

tools.name: etfRotation
tools.description:
Monthly sector-ETF rotation — ranks sector ETFs by 20-day return and applies a regime filter.
Bull regime: top-N long sectors (XLK/XLF/XLE/...). Neutral: top-2 long + 1 bond ETF (BND).
Bear: top-2 inverse ETFs (SH/PSQ) + 1 bond. Companion to concentrated which picks individual
stocks; this picks the diversified ETF for the same sector momentum signal.

topN.description: How many ETFs to hold (default 2; backtest matrix Sharpe peak).

lookbackDays.description: Return lookback window (default 10 trading days; backtest Sharpe 1.41 top-2).

includeInverse.description: Include inverse ETFs (SH/PSQ/SQQQ/SPXU) for bear-regime hedges.

tools.name: cryptoRankingMomentum
tools.description:
Crypto relative-momentum scanner — buys coins climbing the rankings.
Reads top-100 coins by market cap from CoinGecko, ranks them by 7-day
return, returns the top-N (default 5) with their live price, mcap, and
returns. Skips stablecoins and wrapped tokens. Caller pipes through
positionSizer({ strategy: 'crypto_ranking', accountId: 'kraken-paper' }).
Backtest: +295.98% / Sharpe 0.83 / WR 35.8% / 204 trades over 12 months
(beat BTC HODL by ~318 pp).

topUniverseN.description: How many top-mcap coins to scan (CoinGecko returns 100 in one call).

holdings.description: How many top-by-return coins to return as candidates.

lookbackDays.description: Lookback window in days for ranking by return (default 7 = backward-compatible; sweep best was 10).

minReturn7d.description: Floor on lookback return (decimal). Defaults to 0 — accept any positive momentum. Field name kept for back-compat.

tools.name: equityMomentumScanner
tools.description:
Equity 5-day momentum scanner — bull-regime entries on the curated large-cap universe.
ENTRY when:
- 5-day return >= minReturn5d (default 3%)
- 10-day return > 0 (direction confirms)
- close > SMA(200) (uptrend filter)
- RSI(14) < maxRsi (default 75; avoid overbought)
- volume > 20-day avg (above-average participation)
- close > $5 (no penny stocks)
Returns candidates ranked by 5-day return descending. Caller layers
regime gate (bull-only) and ATR trailing stop.

symbols.description: Explicit symbol override. When provided, replaces the curated universe.

tools.name: equityMacdScanner
tools.description:
Equity MACD-crossover scanner. ENTRY when MACD(12,26,9) crosses above signal AND hist > 0 AND close > EMA(200).
Universe defaults to the curated ~120 large-cap RUNNER_UNIVERSE.
Returns ranked candidates. Caller layers regime gate (bull-only) and ATR trail.
Backtest: +32.59% / Sharpe 1.24 / WR 46.8% / 139 trades / 28 months / 1173 stocks (auto-quant).

symbols.description: Override the curated universe with explicit symbols.

tools.name: equityDonchianScanner
tools.description:
Equity Donchian (Turtle) breakout scanner. ENTRY when close > prior 20-day highest high.
Universe defaults to the curated RUNNER_UNIVERSE. Returns ranked candidates.
Caller layers regime gate (bull-only) and ATR trail. Exit guidance: close < 10-day low.
Backtest: +67.80% / Sharpe 0.88 / WR 41.5% / 195 trades / 28 months / 1173 stocks (auto-quant).

tools.name: equityIchimokuScanner
tools.description:
Equity Ichimoku Cloud TK-cross scanner. ENTRY when tenkan(9) crosses above kijun(26) AND close is above the cloud (max of senkouA/B plotted 26 bars ago).
Universe defaults to the curated RUNNER_UNIVERSE. Long-only.
Caller layers regime gate (bull-only) and exits when TK cross reverses.
Backtest: +29.81% / Sharpe 1.49 (ensemble period) / WR 46.5% / 129 trades / 1173 stocks (auto-quant).

symbols.description: Override the curated universe with explicit symbols.

tools.name: rsi2MeanReversion
tools.description:
RSI(2) extreme mean-reversion scanner. ENTRY when RSI(2) < entryThreshold AND close > EMA(200) (uptrend filter).
Returns ranked candidates (most oversold first). Caller stages a limit buy + sets a 1.5× ATR stop and exits when RSI(2) > exitThreshold or 10 days pass.
Backtest: Sharpe 0.76 / +0.95% avg / 38,916 trades / 1.5× ATR stop critical.

symbols.description: Override the curated universe with explicit symbols.

tools.name: insiderBuyingTracker
tools.description: Track recent insider buying (Form 4 purchases via openinsider.com). C-suite + Directors only, $100K minimum. Returns ranked signals with confidence-boost classification: LARGE_CLUSTER / LARGE / CLUSTER / SINGLE. Backtest: +3.31% alpha at 30d (t=6.95) on 3,454 signals. Use as confirmation overlay, not a standalone trigger.

daysBack.description: Lookback window in days for filings.

largeThreshold.description: Threshold above which a buy is classified LARGE.

tools.name: gapFadeFilter
tools.description: Pre-market scan for stocks gapping up >= 15%. Backtest: these fade 62% of the time. Returns a list of symbols to AVOID buying today. Defensive filter, NOT a trading signal. Persists to data/gap-flags.json so the confidence engine penalises flagged names automatically. Run at 08:00 ET and 09:35 ET daily.

tools.name: rankSignals
tools.description:
Rank pending entry signals by expected value and apply concentration filters.

INPUT: an array of { symbol, strategy, details? } from any scanner.
OUTPUT: { accepted, rejected, ranked, summary } — accepted is the list
to actually stage entries for, in priority order. rejected explains why
each non-accepted candidate was filtered (industry-group, strategy-cap,
gap-fade-flag, regime-bear-max, equity-total-cap, or crypto-separate-pool).

Strategy EV ranking (highest first): concentrated > etf_rotation > equity_donchian
> equity_macd > rsi2_reversion > equity_ichimoku > pead > overnight_drift.

Use this AFTER calling all scanners and BEFORE staging any entries.

signals.description: All pending signals from all scanners. Tag each with the originating strategy.

currentPositions.description: Live position list. If omitted, reads from data/state/strategy-positions.json.

regime.description: Current regime. If omitted, reads lastRegime from data/state/pulse-state.json.

tools.name: positionSizer
tools.description:
Compute the exact position size for a trade. Call this BEFORE staging any order.
Equities draw from a shared $4K cash pool (per-position base = available cash / remaining slots
for the strategy). Crypto uses a separate $1K Kraken pool with 8 slots. Returns dollar amount,
share count, full sizing breakdown, and a human rationale. If `status === 'rejected'`, do not
place the trade.

strategy.description: Which strategy this trade belongs to. Drives slot count and Kelly fraction.

accountId.description: Account to size against. Equities default to alpaca-paper, crypto to kraken-paper.

tools.name: narrativeWeekly
tools.description: Rebuild the narrative dislocation map. Identifies confirmed themes (9B), traverses causal chains (Sonnet), verifies + flow/attention-filters via code, writes data/state/narrative-map.json + data/brain/narrative-journal.md. Should run weekly Sunday 22 UTC.

tools.name: narrativeDaily
tools.description: Apply the narrative map to the paper book: refresh entry/exit decisions for up to 8 paper positions, $150 each. Exits on attention arrival (>5 co-mentions), flow gap close (>=80%), theme off map, -15% stop, or 60d max hold. Records closed trades in the trade ledger with strategy='narrative'.

tools.name: narrativeMap
tools.description: Read the current narrative dislocation map + paper book. Use for status checks without rebuilding the map.

symbol.description: If set, return only the candidate row for this symbol (cross-confirmation lookup).

tools.name: strategyAttribution
tools.description:
Per-strategy realized P&L over a trailing window.
- action='read' → group existing ledger rows by strategy (no network)
- action='reconcile' → pull new Alpaca FILL activities + extend ledger
- action='full' (default) → reconcile then read

Returns: { windowDays, strategies: [{strategy, trades, realizedPnL, winRate, avgHoldDays}], totalRealized, untaggedPnL, reconcileStats?}.
Untagged P&L is itself a diagnostic — code paths that bypass strategy tagging show up here.

accountId.description: Only alpaca-paper is supported for the reconcile step today; read covers all accounts.

tools.name: systemStatus
tools.description:
Server hardware status: CPU, memory, disk, uptime, load average, GPU, running containers, and key systemd services.
No inputs. Returns a snapshot of host health.

tools.name: ollamaStatus
tools.description:
Ollama health: version, currently loaded model(s) with VRAM usage, available models in catalog, and the OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL setting.
Returns status='offline' if the server can't be reached.

tools.name: processHealth
tools.description: Health of Hydra-related processes: openalice + ollama + kraken + nginx, with PID, RSS memory, and recent errors from journalctl. Plus disk usage of key data directories.

tools.name: newsSentimentScanner
tools.description:
Score news headlines for stocks via Ollama, aggregate to sectors.

Modes:
  - 'full' (default): score the ~150 scheduledScoring symbols, write data/sentiment.json. ~3min.
  - 'single': score ONE symbol on demand (e.g. when MACD/Donchian fires on a name not in the 150). ~3s. Persists.
  - 'sector': score every symbol in one ETF bucket. ~3-5min depending on bucket size. Persists.
  - 'all':    score every symbol in the 1173 universe. ~20min. Persists.

Reads sector mapping from data/sector-universe.json (built by scripts/build_sector_universe.py).

symbol.description: Required when mode=single — the ticker to score.

etf.description: Required when mode=sector — the SPDR ETF (XLK, XLF, ...) of the sector to scan.
